mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in housing starts signals a supply-side shock that compresses housing prices and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-percentage-point increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation rise in real GDP signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that compresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5-percentage-point decrease in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decline in the federal funds rate signals a easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing monetary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
